mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, labor market slack signals severe demand destruction and recessionary pressures, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 9.0, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio exceeds 0.5 during these periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and stable consumer price indices indicate robust consumer spending without inflationary overheating, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in >60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during post-shock recovery phases reflects rapid capacity utilization and earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, with mean forward returns below zero or t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening economic activity and higher corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 20-day forward returns over rolling 2-year windows, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly consumer price index increases exceeding 0.5 percent trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >50% of high-inflation months, or the signal shows no predictive power (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and real GDP signals synchronized expansion across residential and aggregate demand sectors, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in >65% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during late-cycle expansions signals capacity constraints and impending economic slowdown, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late cycles fail to predict negative 5-day returns, with mean forward returns above zero or out-of-sample accuracy below 50%.
